text.title: Testing with Assistive Technology Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Testing with Assistive Technology methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each finding pins AT (NVDA / JAWS / VoiceOver / TalkBack / Switch Control / Voice Control / Magnifier) + version + device + OS.

rationale.source: WebAIM SR survey + AppleVision a11y notes

rule.rationale: AT versions differ in announcement and behaviour.

rule.statement: Test matrix MUST cover at least 2 ATs per critical flow (e.g. SR desktop + SR mobile; voice + switch).
Each test run MUST attach a recording or transcript (Guidepup log / OBS clip / WindowsAT trace).
Each finding maps to at least one WCAG SC; AT-only observations without SC mapping are tagged `informational`.
rule.rationale: SC mapping enables aggregation across audit layers.

rule.statement: AT rigs MUST not be modified mid-cycle (no OS updates, no AT updates). Updates require a documented re-baseline.
rule.rationale: Rig changes mid-cycle invalidate prior findings.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-testing-with-assistive-technology.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
